text.title: API Gateway Core Rules
text.summary: Stateless gateway rules: auth, rate limiting, request tracing, timeouts, circuit breakers, and the smart-gateway antipattern.

rule.statement: Gateways handle only cross-cutting concerns: auth (JWT/mTLS), rate limiting, routing, CORS, tracing headers, and SSL termination. Never put business logic, data transformation, or domain rules in gateway plugins.
rule.rationale: Business logic in gateway plugins (Lua, JS, WASM) creates a new monolith owned by the platform team, invisible to domain developers, and blocked by a different release cycle.

rule.statement: Gateway must be stateless. Auth state lives in tokens (JWT, mTLS certs). Rate-limit counters live in Redis. Never store session state in the gateway process.
rule.rationale: Stateful gateways cannot be horizontally scaled and become a single point of failure for all traffic.

rule.statement: Rate-limit by consumer (API key or JWT sub), not by source IP. When behind a CDN or load balancer, source IP is the CDN's IP — all consumers share the same limit bucket.
rule.rationale: IP-based rate limiting behind a CDN applies the limit to all consumers collectively, not individually. Consumer-based limiting is fair and attributable.

rule.statement: Inject X-Request-ID at ingress ($request_id in nginx, correlation-id plugin in Kong). Propagate the header to all upstream services. Log the ID on every gateway response.
rule.rationale: Without a correlation ID, tracing a failed request across 5 microservices requires timestamp matching in logs — unreliable under concurrent traffic.

rule.statement: Set gateway timeout shorter than client timeout: if client waits 10s, set gateway to 5s. This ensures the gateway returns 504 cleanly before the client disconnects.
rule.rationale: If the gateway timeout equals or exceeds the client timeout, clients abort before receiving the gateway's 504 response, losing error context.

rule.statement: Configure circuit breakers per upstream service. A slow upstream must not degrade gateway response times for all other services.
rule.rationale: Without isolation, one slow upstream causes thread/connection pool exhaustion in the gateway, degrading all routes.

rule.statement: All gateway configuration must be versioned in git and deployed via CI. Never change routes or plugins via a web UI without a matching PR.
rule.rationale: Web UI changes are unreviewed, unversioned, and unreproducible. A mis-click can take down all traffic with no rollback path.
